title: The Ancestral Release and Wealth Lineage Activation
subtitle: A ritual for closing the door on inherited struggle and opening the path to generational abundance
excerpt: A ten-minute ritual from Week 2 of The Money Journal. The ceremony moves through five stages: prepare, release, allow, integrate, and anchor. It marks the close of the generational work and names you as the turning point in your family's money story.
sourceNotes: The Week 2 Ritual: The Ancestral Release & Wealth Lineage Activation from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: The Week 2 closing ceremony from The Money Journal. The ritual has five stages and takes around ten minutes. It marks the close of the generational-struggle work and names you specifically as the turning point in your family's money story.
New to rituals as a practice? Read
how rituals work
first.
The practice
Prepare

Sit quietly somewhere you won't be interrupted. Take a few slow breaths. Picture your family line standing behind you: parents, grandparents, ancestors. Each one places a hand gently on your shoulder. They are not a weight; they are with you.
Release (repeat x3)
I am ready to release all fear, struggle, and scarcity passed down through my family line. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
Allow (repeat x3)
I am ready to align with and allow ease, wealth, and stability for myself and every generation that follows. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Integrate

Take a slow breath in and all the way out. Picture golden light moving through your family line, backwards through the generations who came before, forward through the ones who will come after. Backwards and forward, settling in the present.
Anchor
This week, I am grateful that I am the turning point in my family's story of wealth.
Write this sentence in your own handwriting. Say it out loud. Let it stand as a record of the week.
Where this practice comes from
The Week 2 Ritual from
The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow
(Rebecca J Page, 2025). The five-part ceremony structure (prepare, release, allow, integrate, anchor) is original to the journal.
